mechanism_narrative: A persistently high unemployment rate signals a weak labor market, which constrains consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This reflects a general economic malaise.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where UNRATE is above 7.0% over a 12-month average.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises above a critical threshold, it signals potential monetary tightening by the Fed, increasing discount rates and negatively impacting equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the high inflation signal.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of strong retail sales (RSAFS) and robust housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand, driving corporate earnings growth and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND HOUST 3-month average growth > 5%
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST show strong growth, SPX 20d returns are negative more often than positive over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recession recovery regime, sustained job creation (PAYEMS) indicates improving economic health and consumer confidence, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During identified post-recession recovery periods, strong PAYEMS growth does not correlate with positive 60d SPX returns (Rank IC < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) growth consistently exceeds a moderate threshold, it signals a healthy and expanding economy, which typically leads to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2.5% for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of periods where GDPC1 growth consistently exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces borrowing costs for businesses and consumers, stimulating economic activity and supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for more than 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in industrial production (INDPRO) and a decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies a robust and efficient supply side meeting growing demand, leading to strong corporate performance and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month average growth > 0.3% AND UNRATE 3-month average change < -0.1pp
failure_condition: When INDPRO is rising and UNRATE is falling, SPX 20d returns are not significantly positive (t-statistic < 1.5) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and impending economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by > 0.5 percentage points over any 3-month period
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months.
